summary: Fixed systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES by copying DistrictControlState at Query v1 and StateDelta read-model boundaries. Added regression coverage proving mutating returned district summary and delta control DTOs cannot mutate runtime.world or invalidate WorldState invariants.

tests_run.result: Pre-fix regression run failed as expected: mutating state.district-control-updated delta control changed runtime.world.state.districts[0].control.controllerPolityId from 1 to 999.
Test Files 5 passed (5); Tests 27 passed (27). Includes new alias isolation regression for query and delta control DTOs.
Node hash: 61e0ba1c; Worker-compatible hash: 61e0ba1c; Node command/query hash: d275faa4; Worker-compatible command/query hash: d275faa4; Deterministic command/query simulation matched.
Architecture boundaries passed. Self-test fixture produced 15 expected violation(s).
architecture:check and sim:determinism passed; root Vitest Test Files 8 passed (8), Tests 33 passed (33); web Vitest Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 3 passed (3).
pnpm -r --if-present typecheck completed successfully, including sim-core and web.
ESLint completed with no reported errors.

decisions: Added copyDistrictControlState as the explicit DistrictControlState DTO boundary helper.
WorldState, StateDelta, and Query read-model district control objects now receive separate object instances.
No command semantics, protocol schema, scheduler order, RNG, save schema, task status, or forbidden paths were changed.
risks: R3 remains active because SIM-003 command/query authority behavior still requires systems_architect review.
This fix covers the current nested DistrictControlState DTO; future read-model DTOs with nested objects should use explicit copy helpers at the boundary.
requested_action: Review the alias isolation fix and rerun/inspect the added regression coverage for Query v1 and StateDelta read-model boundary safety.
